title: For Whom The Wedding Bells Toll

jokes.text: I got to marry Lisa more often.
jokes.setup: Will is excited about receiving wedding gifts
jokes.punchline: Suggests marrying his fiancée repeatedly just for the gifts
jokes.explanation: Quick, economical joke that shows Will's materialistic side - classic character comedy

jokes.text: Well, ho, ho, ho.
jokes.setup: Will compares gift-opening to Christmas
jokes.punchline: Carlton delivers deadpan Santa reference
jokes.explanation: Basic setup-punchline with Carlton's characteristic literalness

jokes.text: What do you think was in that one? Something that has to be returned.
jokes.setup: Glass breaking sound effect suggests broken gift
jokes.punchline: Carlton's matter-of-fact response about having to return it
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about wedding gift practicalities

jokes.text: My favorite wedding guest. Ben Franklin. Oh, excuse me, Ben. Allow me to show you to your seat.
jokes.setup: Will finds money envelopes in wedding gifts
jokes.punchline: Treats $100 bill like a person, formally seating it
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Will's charm and materialism, with creative personification of money

jokes.text: Oh, rent a room.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil and Aunt Viv are being romantic
jokes.punchline: Will's disgusted reaction to his guardians' PDA
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction to adults being romantic - functional but predictable

jokes.text: I don't know. Can he take a punch? I'm kidding, I'm kidding. No, seriously, can he?
jokes.setup: Will asks if Lisa's dad will get along with his mom
jokes.punchline: Lisa pretends to joke about violence, then doubles down
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection structure - acts like she's backing down then reveals she's serious

jokes.text: Get off of me, woman!
jokes.setup: Lisa is being affectionate before her father arrives
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated rejection when he hears footsteps
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy about being caught in compromising position - standard sitcom fare

jokes.text: He'd say, you ain't fooling nobody.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks what her dad would say about their obvious romance
jokes.punchline: Will admits their relationship is transparent
jokes.explanation: Honest admission that gets a laugh through Will's self-awareness

jokes.text: Oh, man, I know how you feel. Lisa do that to me all the time. Man, just last night, we...
jokes.setup: Fred complains about airplane passenger stealing his blanket
jokes.punchline: Will overshares about intimate details with Lisa's father
jokes.explanation: Classic Will oversharing moment - good character work and awkwardness

jokes.text: Oh, I'm just practicing for the honeymoon night. Yo, bellhop! If the room be bopping, don't you be knocking.
jokes.setup: Lisa finds Will doing push-ups
jokes.punchline: Will's rhyming sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with Will's signature rhyming style and sexual bravado

jokes.text: You! You! Hey, hey. Chunky butt, meet battleaxe. Battleaxe, chunky butt.
jokes.setup: Both parents realize they were each other's annoying airplane seatmate
jokes.punchline: Will formally introduces them using their mutual insults
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the setup airplane complaints - perfect comedic timing and structure

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. You look like a chocolate-flavored Pepto-Bismol.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks Will to compliment Ashley in her bridesmaid dress
jokes.punchline: Will's unflattering but creative comparison
jokes.explanation: Creative visual metaphor that's both specific and insulting in Will's signature style

jokes.text: No problem. To put over my head.
jokes.setup: Ashley wants a matching bag for her ugly dress
jokes.punchline: Reveals she wants to hide her face, not accessorize
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation of fashion request, delivers self-deprecating punchline

jokes.text: Okay. Come on, Ike and Tina, let's go.
jokes.setup: Parents are clearly antagonistic toward each other
jokes.punchline: Will compares them to famously volatile couple Ike and Tina Turner
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that perfectly captures the dynamic between the feuding parents

jokes.text: Okay, tubby, tuck in your gut, we're going downtown.
jokes.setup: Vy trying to be nice as requested
jokes.punchline: Her version of 'nice' is still insulting Fred
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Vy's idea of being nice

jokes.text: In Philly, 'tubby' mean cute bald man.
jokes.setup: Vy calls Fred 'tubby' in an insulting way
jokes.punchline: Will tries to defuse by claiming regional meaning difference
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Will's peacemaking attempts with his signature Philadelphia references

jokes.text: Just don't be late. You might find her face-down in the crab cake.
jokes.setup: Will is leaving the feuding parents alone at dinner
jokes.punchline: Fred suggests Vy might murder him
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their conflict with specific, visual imagery

jokes.text: I don't know what he's complaining about. I like my women scrappy.
jokes.setup: Fred just expressed fear about Vy's aggression
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by saying he likes tough women
jokes.explanation: Character contradiction that shows Fred's mixed feelings about Vy's aggression

jokes.text: I didn't say it was a good plan.
jokes.setup: Will explains his plan to leave parents alone, then they drive off fighting
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry comment on the plan's immediate failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - dry, understated commentary on the chaos around him

jokes.text: Oh, man. You took your gold tooth out. What's the occasion?
jokes.setup: Jazz appears without his signature gold tooth
jokes.punchline: Will treats the absence of bling as formal occasion wear
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Jazz's style choices being tied to his emotional state

jokes.text: The wife and I are split up. Inconsolable differences.
jokes.setup: Will asks about Jazz's missing gold tooth
jokes.punchline: Jazz mispronounces 'irreconcilable' as 'inconsolable'
jokes.explanation: Standard malapropism joke showing Jazz's lack of education

jokes.text: And so did the weave.
jokes.setup: Jazz says the marriage mask came off
jokes.punchline: Literally - his wife's hair weave came off too
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from metaphorical to literal 'mask coming off'

jokes.text: Hortense? Jazz, I thought her name was Jewel. Just one of her many lies.
jokes.setup: Jazz reveals his wife's real name
jokes.punchline: Even her name was fake, treating it as another deception
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of Jazz's marriage horror story with absurd treatment of name change

jokes.text: She had sex with all of them? She did? Man, it just gets worse and worse.
jokes.setup: Jazz reveals wife slept with everyone in building
jokes.punchline: Will's escalating horror at each revelation
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction comedy to increasingly outrageous revelations

jokes.text: What the hell is that? Hi. See? I knew she was gonna say that.
jokes.setup: Will claims to know Lisa better than himself, pointing at his hand
jokes.punchline: Has mysterious mark on hand he doesn't recognize, then claims he predicted Lisa's greeting
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag undermining Will's confidence about knowing people, with quick recovery attempt

jokes.text: Or one of them's dead and one of them's a fugitive.
jokes.setup: Parents haven't called from dinner
jokes.punchline: Lisa's darkly comic explanation for their silence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation about their parents' feud taken to logical extreme

jokes.text: At least she ain't got no weave.
jokes.setup: Jazz mentions Hortense took the car in divorce
jokes.punchline: Will finds silver lining in earlier weave revelation
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke with Will trying to console Jazz by finding positive spin

jokes.text: Baby, I hate sweet potato pie.
jokes.setup: Will confidently claims Lisa loves sweet potato pie
jokes.punchline: Lisa reveals she actually hates it
jokes.explanation: First crack in Will's confidence about knowing Lisa, setting up larger realization

jokes.text: But personally, I think the guy's a lowlife slug whose whole sexual identity is an extension of his big, stupid gun.
jokes.setup: Lisa admits she doesn't like Shaft movies
jokes.punchline: Delivers sophisticated film criticism about masculine overcompensation
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated humor showing Lisa's intelligence contrasting with Will's assumption about her tastes

jokes.text: I think he's gay. Ah!
jokes.setup: Lisa's criticism of Shaft character
jokes.punchline: Final revelation that completely undermines Will's assumptions, plus his shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Escalation that completely undermines Will's confidence about knowing his fiancée

jokes.text: Your name is Lisa, isn't it? Well, it is now. I just couldn't go through life hearing people say, 'Hey, Beula.'
jokes.setup: Will's growing panic about not knowing Lisa leads to basic name question
jokes.punchline: Lisa reveals she changed her name from the old-fashioned 'Beula'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Will's crisis and good character detail about Lisa's self-reinvention

jokes.text: Bye, Beula.
jokes.setup: Lisa just revealed her real name was Beula
jokes.punchline: Will uses her old name as she leaves
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that shows Will processing the name revelation with characteristic irreverence

jokes.text: [SCREAMING]
jokes.setup: Will walks in on his mother and Fred in bed
jokes.punchline: Will's horrified screaming reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard 'walked in on parents' physical comedy - gets reaction but isn't sophisticated

jokes.text: Will. [WILL SCREAMING] You, him, here. I need a moment.
jokes.setup: Will catching his mother in bed with Fred
jokes.punchline: Vy's matter-of-fact response to Will's trauma
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment, Vy's casual response contrasts with Will's trauma

jokes.text: Mamma, no!
jokes.setup: Will is trying to process what he saw
jokes.punchline: Repeated traumatized outbursts
jokes.explanation: Continued trauma reaction - functional but predictable sitcom territory

jokes.text: What's this, a pajama party? Hey, scooch over.
jokes.setup: Family gathers in Will's room after the screaming
jokes.punchline: Carlton treats the crisis like a fun sleepover
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - completely missing the emotional weight of the situation

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I just got a mental picture! Get out! Get out!
jokes.setup: Will is trying to explain his trauma to his family
jokes.punchline: Gets re-traumatized by remembering what he saw
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the trauma reaction - gets laughs but is fairly standard

jokes.text: Oh, I know where they are, baby, You left them on top of my... Maya Angelou's book of poetry. Come on, I'll show you where they are.
jokes.setup: Vy is looking for her earrings
jokes.punchline: Fred almost reveals intimate location, covers with awkward book reference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing the parents' continued romantic activity with awkward cover-up

jokes.text: I said I was listening. But I got a million things to do, so hurry.
jokes.setup: Will desperately needs to talk, Phil says he's listening
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he's too busy and wants Will to rush
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's well-meaning but distracted parenting style

jokes.text: Remember when you said we was too young to get married? When I told you mind your business, I know what I'm doing? You remember how wrong I always am?
jokes.setup: Will is seeking advice about his doubts
jokes.punchline: Admits his pattern of being wrong about everything
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware character moment where Will acknowledges his flaws with humor

jokes.text: Go in there, say I do, be done with it. Feel better? Well... Good. Okay. Posse out, let's go.
jokes.setup: Will has serious doubts about marriage
jokes.punchline: Phil gives dismissive advice and immediately leaves
jokes.explanation: Phil's characteristically unhelpful but confident advice delivered with comic efficiency

jokes.text: Tell me about it. I've got to balance a ring on a stupid pillow. Hey, you wanna talk about it? No.
jokes.setup: Will says wedding stuff is stressing him out
jokes.punchline: Nicky's mundane ring bearer concerns, then immediate rejection of conversation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing even the youngest family member has no time for Will's crisis

jokes.text: Oh, Lisa. This dress looks almost as beautiful on you as it would on me.
jokes.setup: Hilary is complimenting Lisa on her wedding dress
jokes.punchline: Manages to make it about herself and her own beauty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - turns compliment into self-aggrandizement with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Well, make it fast. If I start to frizz, you're a dead man.
jokes.setup: Will wants to talk to Lisa before wedding despite superstition
jokes.punchline: Hilary's threat about hair maintenance being life-or-death
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary prioritizing appearance over everything else with dramatic threat

jokes.text: Hey, hey, girl! This ain't no time to be getting freaky. Will, I was trying to hold your hand. Oh, yeah? Well, that wasn't it.
jokes.setup: Will and Lisa are trying to have serious conversation without looking at each other
jokes.punchline: Lisa accidentally grabs wrong body part while trying to hold his hand
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy from the awkwardness of not looking at each other - gets laughs but is predictable

jokes.text: You know, as hard as this is to believe, I'm gonna miss you.
jokes.setup: Carlton is giving Will a pre-wedding sendoff
jokes.punchline: Acts like Will is dying rather than just getting married
jokes.explanation: Carlton's characteristic over-dramatization of normal life events

jokes.text: Yes, I... Whoo! ...do. But I don't.
jokes.setup: Will starts to say traditional wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Interrupts himself with celebration then reveals he doesn't want to marry
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts wedding expectations with perfect comedic timing and structure

jokes.text: Hey, looks like we finally agree on something.
jokes.setup: Both Will and Lisa deciding not to get married
jokes.punchline: Will noting this is their first agreement, referencing earlier flower scene
jokes.explanation: Great callback to earlier episode moment, bittersweet irony

jokes.text: Hey, looks like we finally agree on something.
jokes.setup: Will and Lisa both decide not to get married
jokes.punchline: Points out the irony that their first agreement is to break up
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about relationships with good ironic twist

jokes.text: Well, save it for that 50 pounds of shrimp swimming around on that damn ice swan.
jokes.setup: Will announces there won't be a wedding
jokes.punchline: Mentions elaborate reception preparations going to waste
jokes.explanation: Good visual imagery about wasteful wedding preparations with Will's characteristic pragmatism

jokes.text: We met at 10,000 feet in the air. And I promise you, baby, if you marry me, neither of us will ever come down.
jokes.setup: Fred proposes to Vy referencing their airplane meeting
jokes.punchline: Romantic metaphor about staying high on love
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback to their meet-cute with romantic wordplay that reframes their conflict

jokes.text: No. No, Mom, no. Absolutely not. You said that me and Lisa were rushing too fast. Y'all gotta take some time to think about this.
jokes.setup: Will just called off his own rushed wedding
jokes.punchline: Immediately lectures parents about rushing into marriage
jokes.explanation: Great ironic reversal where Will becomes the voice of reason after his own relationship failure

jokes.text: Boy, I ain't got that much time. I'm getting old. Now move.
jokes.setup: Will wants parents to take more time to think about marriage
jokes.punchline: Vy's practical response about her age and urgency
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Vy's no-nonsense attitude and practical approach to romance

jokes.text: Rap on, Rev.
jokes.setup: Will gives up trying to stop his mother's wedding
jokes.punchline: Tells reverend to proceed with hip-hop slang
jokes.explanation: Classic Will mixing street slang with formal situations - good character consistency

jokes.text: I cannot believe he is back on the market. I mean, you know, I'm sorry for the girl, but who cares? He is so fine.
jokes.setup: Women at the reception hearing about Will's broken engagement
jokes.punchline: Fake sympathy immediately followed by excitement about Will being single
jokes.explanation: Good observation about human nature, shows realistic reaction

jokes.text: I cannot believe he is back on the market. I mean, you know, I'm sorry for the girl, but who cares? He is so fine.
jokes.setup: Wedding guests gossiping about Will calling off wedding
jokes.punchline: Fake sympathy immediately replaced by excitement about his availability
jokes.explanation: Standard social commentary about people's shallow priorities at others' expense

jokes.text: Wait, wait... No. No. Wait a... Wait a minute. Wait a minute, now. This is... This is all just too soon. Now, September, that's a different story. Y'all come back, now, you hear?
jokes.setup: Will realizes his mother is actually getting married
jokes.punchline: Tries to postpone it to September like a business meeting
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Will's continued discomfort with the speed while treating it like scheduling conflict

tmdb_overview: Husband-to-be Will has the bride, the ring, the date...and second thoughts about the whole idea.
